_thread: Thread A — DB Connection Pool Exhaustion
_notes: Clean incident thread. Schema fills well. See gap notes below.
title: DB Connection Pool Exhaustion — Summary Enrichment Service Deployment
summary: A deployment of the summary-enrichment service at 08:45 caused a connection leak that exhausted the Oracle Universal Connection Pool (UCP, max 20 connections), resulting in a ~41-minute incident with 12% error rate on /api/summary. The service was rolled back to v2.3.1. Root cause was a missing connection.close() call inside an enrichment loop in SummaryEnrichmentService.java line 87. PR #442 contains the permanent fix; UCP max pool size increased to 40 as a short-term buffer.
steps_taken: Identified Oracle UCP pool exhaustion — 20 connections borrowed, 150 requests queued
Correlated spike with 08:45 deployment of summary-enrichment service
Rolled back summary-enrichment service to v2.3.1
Confirmed UCP stats and error rate recovering post-rollback
Identified root cause: missing connection.close() in SummaryEnrichmentService.java line 87
Bumped Oracle UCP max pool size from 20 to 40 as short-term buffer
resolution: Rolled back summary-enrichment service to v2.3.1. Permanent fix in PR #442. Oracle UCP max pool size increased to 40 pending PR merge.
root_cause: Missing connection.close() in SummaryEnrichmentService.java line 87 — the service opened a new Oracle DB connection per request inside a loop without returning it to the pool.
action_items: Revert Oracle UCP max pool size from 40 back to 20 after PR #442 is merged and verified

resolved_from_v1: severity now populated (p2 — 12% error rate, 41 min, no data loss)

root_cause remains string (correctly populated for incident type)
